LAW OFFICES 
OF
Hnnmex H. FnzCERALD
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORAIION
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices
428 "J Street, Ste. 800
345 LORTON AVENUE, SUTTE 3O2
Jutv 9. 1990 
""";'f#.J^H',1i.:3:-".i3i"
- -'t 
FAcsrMrLE (4r5) 344-3sra
Jur ,r E al i '39
Commission
Sacramento, California 95804
Re: Request for !nformal Advice
!nterest !ssue
Letter Regarding a Conflict of
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
Our firm represents a city redevelopement agency and we have been
asked to research a potential conflict of interest problem. We seek your
advice in the form of an informal advice letter with respect to the
following issue:
Whether a City Councilman, who also owns a private roofing company
which operates inside and outside the city limits, is precluded from
acting on matters which come before the Council through a paid
representative of certain private developers for redevelopemnt projects
within the city limits, where said paid representative is also a private
developer who employs the City Councilman's roofing company for
projects lying outside the city limits?
Facts
City Councilman owns a roofing company which operates inside and
outside the city limits. City Councilman's roofing company has performecl
construction services to a Private Developer for projects lying outside the
city limits. City Councilman has received more than 9250.00 in income
from the Private Developer within the last 12 months.
Private Developer is also a paid representative of various other
private developers who from time to time request city approval from the
council for projects within the city. Note also that said private
Developer is an independant contractor who has a contract with the city


as a consultant on redevelopment projects.
We hereby request an informal advice letter regarding the City
Councilman's position under the Political Reform Act of 1974 and any
potential conflict of interest problem with respect to the above scenario.
Thank you for your anticipated cooperation.
Very truly yours,
--0/,*e; C %,-"4UU
CHHISTINE C. FITZGERALD

Thank you for your anticipated cooperation.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a City Councilman with a roofing company can participate in Council decisions involving developers who have hired the Councilman's company for projects outside the city, where one developer serves as a paid representative of other developers seeking city approval.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The letter is from a law firm (Honnex H. Fitzgerald) seeking advice from the FPPC. Document contains Facts section detailing the scenario but no CONCLUSION or ADVICE sections from FPPC. Date is July 9, 1990. Minor OCR artifacts in header but core content is clear.
